steps.title: Use the isosceles condition
steps.content: Since $RT = TU$, triangle $RTU$ is isosceles, so its base angles are equal: $\angle TRU = \angle TUR$. The exterior angle at $T$ (marked $114^{\circ}$) is supplementary to the interior angle $\angle RTU$, so $\angle RTU = 180^{\circ} - 114^{\circ} = 66^{\circ}$. Therefore each base angle is $\frac{180^{\circ} - 66^{\circ}}{2} = 57^{\circ}$.

steps.title: Find the angles at R from triangle STR
steps.content: The exterior angle of triangle $STR$ at vertex $T$ equals the sum of the two non-adjacent interior angles: $114^{\circ} = \angle SRT + \angle RST$. From the figure, $\angle RST = 50^{\circ}$, so $\angle SRT = 114^{\circ} - 50^{\circ} = 64^{\circ}$.

steps.title: Identify x
steps.content: From the figure, $x = \angle SRT = 64^{\circ}$. The answer is 64 — that matches C.
